title: Frontend Runtime Integration Readiness
purpose: Prepare the FastAPI parent backend for safe browser-based local integration with the separate React/Vite frontend by adding explicit credentialed CORS support, preserving and documenting the HTTP-only cookie auth contract, grounding frontend-facing route inventory in current repo reality, adding runtime OpenAPI surface regression coverage, and requiring compile plus Docker verification before completion. This spec is backend-only and must not implement frontend clients, React/Vite changes, or any new commercial/payment truth inside the parent backend.

context.summary: The prior frontend/backend contract-alignment workstream is complete and the backend already exposes a broad authenticated /api/v1 surface for auth, settings, dashboard, launcher, billing, support, announcements, service status, rewards, and Discord integrations. The remaining gap before frontend runtime integration is not broad API implementation; it is browser runtime readiness, especially explicit non-wildcard credentialed CORS, durable documentation of the cookie-session browser contract, OpenAPI/runtime regression coverage, and backend verification discipline.
repo_reality_summary: app/main.py currently creates the FastAPI app, registers request-id middleware and exception handlers, and mounts the versioned router at settings.api_v1_prefix, but it does not yet register FastAPI CORSMiddleware.

app/core/config.py already centralizes runtime settings for api_v1_prefix and auth cookie behavior, including auth_session_cookie_name=zeptalytic_session, auth_session_cookie_path=/, auth_session_cookie_samesite=lax, and auth_session_cookie_secure=False, but it does not yet expose dedicated CORS settings.

app/api/routers/v1/__init__.py includes the implemented auth, profiles, addresses, communication-preferences, support, announcements, service-status, rewards, dashboard, launcher, billing, and integrations routers, confirming the browser-facing route inventory already exists in runtime code.
app/api/routers/v1/auth.py sets and clears the zeptalytic_session cookie with httponly=True and configured secure/samesite/path values, which matches the documented HTTP-only browser session model.

tests/unit/test_auth_api.py already verifies safe auth/session payloads, cookie-setting behavior on signup/login/password rotation, cookie clearing on logout and session revocation, and that the browser-facing contract uses the zeptalytic_session cookie rather than raw token responses.

tests/unit/test_router_registration.py already locks the implemented /api/v1 route surface across auth, settings, support, rewards, dashboard, launcher, billing, and integrations, so this workstream should build on that inventory instead of inventing new endpoints.

docs/architecture/Frontend_Backend_Contract_Inventory.md already documents the implemented authenticated/account-facing route surface, making it the likely backend-owned route inventory artifact to refine rather than introducing an unrelated second inventory document unless repo reality requires one.

The runtime integration guide already documents the required local Vite origins http://localhost:5173 and http://127.0.0.1:5173 plus the requirement for frontend requests to use credentials: include, but repo searches found no existing runtime CORS middleware wiring or dedicated OpenAPI regression test file yet.

Repo searches found include_in_schema=False only on hidden _contract helper endpoints for profiles, addresses, communication-preferences, and support; frontend-critical runtime routes remain visible in the normal router surface and should remain represented in OpenAPI unless a documented security reason requires otherwise.

global_guardrails: Search before editing.
Do not expand scope beyond this spec.
Do not add dependencies unless John explicitly approves them.
Do not edit the frontend repo.
Do not create frontend API clients.
Do not modify React/Vite files.
Do not redesign stable frontend pages.
Do not duplicate Pay commercial business rules in parent.
Do not store sensitive payment details in parent.
Do not build admin dashboards unless explicitly scoped.
Do not make Discord linkage affect rewards or product access unless the spec explicitly says so.
Do not allow frontend APIs to directly award points/rewards/badges.
Do not return raw ORM objects from routers.
Use explicit safe DTOs for API responses.
Use explicit allowed origins for credentialed CORS; do not use wildcard credentialed CORS.
Preserve HTTP-only cookie auth.
Do not store raw session tokens in browser-visible responses.
Do not mark incomplete items complete.
Append progress entries to the absolute end of progress/progress.txt.
Run python -m compileall app tests alembic before marking implementation items complete.
Run docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test before marking implementation items complete.

items.title: Add CORS Runtime Settings
items.description: Extend the backend settings/config layer with explicit CORS origin and credential flags so the browser runtime contract is configured centrally rather than hard-coded only in app startup.
acceptance_criteria: The backend settings layer exposes explicit allowed origins for credentialed browser requests.
Defaults include both http://localhost:5173 and http://127.0.0.1:5173.
The settings surface includes allow_credentials=true behavior for the frontend runtime contract.
No wildcard credentialed CORS behavior is introduced.

items.title: Wire FastAPI CORSMiddleware
items.description: Register FastAPI CORSMiddleware using configured settings so local React/Vite browser requests can call the backend with credentials while preserving the existing router and middleware wiring.
acceptance_criteria: app/main.py wires CORSMiddleware from settings rather than an unscoped hard-coded origin list.
Allowed origins are restricted to the configured explicit local frontend origins for this workstream.
allow_credentials is enabled for allowed origins and preflight behavior is covered by tests.
Existing router mounting, request-id middleware, and exception-handler behavior remain intact.

items.title: Verify And Document Browser Cookie Auth Contract
items.description: Verify and document the browser-facing auth/session contract so the frontend integration can rely on HTTP-only cookie sessions, credentials: include, and safe session summary reads without introducing browser-visible token storage.
acceptance_criteria: Repo documentation explicitly states that browser requests must use credentials: include.
The cookie contract remains zeptalytic_session with HTTP-only behavior and configured path/samesite/secure attributes.
Tests or docs confirm login, signup, session, password rotation, logout, and session revocation preserve cookie-session behavior.
No frontend-readable raw session token response contract, localStorage token model, or sessionStorage token model is introduced.

items.title: Document Frontend-Facing Backend Route Inventory
items.description: Refine the backend-owned route inventory from actual repo reality so frontend integration work can reference the current runtime-available /api/v1 surface without inventing unimplemented endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: The inventory documents implemented frontend-facing routes for auth, dashboard, launcher, billing, settings/profile/preferences/addresses, rewards/objectives/badges, support/status/announcements, and Discord integrations where present.
The inventory distinguishes repo-reality implemented routes from future or blocked surfaces rather than inventing missing public-page APIs.
The inventory remains aligned with the canonical router registration surface under app/api/routers/v1 and app/main.py.
The resulting artifact is backend-owned and suitable for frontend integration reference.

items.title: Add OpenAPI Runtime Surface Regression Coverage
items.description: Add regression coverage against app.openapi() so the frontend-critical runtime route surface stays visible and consistent with the documented browser contract.
acceptance_criteria: Tests inspect app.openapi() and verify the core frontend route surface includes auth, dashboard, launcher, billing, settings, rewards, support, announcements, service-status, and integrations paths as implemented.
Frontend-critical runtime paths remain present in OpenAPI unless a documented security reason requires otherwise.
Regression coverage does not depend on live Pay or Discord services.
The documented browser contract and runtime OpenAPI surface remain aligned.

items.title: Run Full Backend Verification
items.description: Run the required backend verification commands after the runtime-readiness items above are implemented and do not mark the workstream complete unless both authoritative checks pass.
acceptance_criteria: python -m compileall app tests alembic passes.
docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test passes.
The active spec is updated honestly based on the verification outcome.
progress/progress.txt is appended at EOF and no frontend repo files are modified.

completion_definition: Relevant tests are added or updated.
python -m compileall app tests alembic passes.
docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test passes.
No unrelated dirty files are introduced.
No frontend repo files are modified.
No incomplete item is marked complete.
